reasoning:
Koneru Humpy's Elo rating on February 1, 2026 is exactly 2535 according to the Wikipedia fetch. To be at least 1% higher, her rating must increase by at least 25.35 points (to 2561+). 

For the first resolution date (February 8, 2026): FIDE publishes its official rating lists monthly (on the 1st of the month), and Wikipedia mirrors these monthly updates. Consequently, the Wikipedia page will not reflect any new official classical chess rating updates by February 8, meaning her rating will remain identical to the February 1 list (2535) and effectively has a 0% chance of gaining 25.35+ points (adjusted to the lower bound 0.05). 

For the second resolution date (March 3, 2026): The Wikipedia page will likely have updated to reflect FIDE's March 1, 2026 rankings. However, it is exceedingly rare for an established elite Super-GM (who is currently playing infrequently and older than her peak rating-growth years) to gain 26 rating points in a single month. Doing so would require a massively dominant structural performance (+26 rating points is typically achieving an 85%+ score in a difficult tournament within the month), which happens in far less than 5% of monthly rating cycles for top-20 women. Therefore, I'm assigning a 0.06 probability for March 3.
